sub_types: Online / Digital Bank / Neobank / Fintech Bank

real_questions: Has the enforcement of federal student loan payments affected you?
Any movement in loan modifications recently or over the past few years?
How do you think about high credit limits — some larger banks have gotten in trouble there?

real_questions.text: How does your data collection compare to large banks?
sub_types: Online / Digital Bank / Neobank / Fintech Bank

real_questions.text: For competitors who also collect data, are credit stats shown for all loans originated or just the ones retained on book?
sub_types: Online / Digital Bank / Neobank / Fintech Bank

real_questions: Your growth in C&I, CRE, or NDFI lending is running against the sector-wide tightening shown in the SLOOS — what underwrites your confidence?
agency_intel: Fitch is harsher than the other agencies on lower-credit-quality assets, and gives less credit when banks move risk into securitization or warehousing structures.
Fitch is currently watching federal student loan repayment stress and high-credit-limit consumer card exposure (driven by recent issues at larger banks).

agency_intel.text: The retained-vs.-originated credit-stat distinction is a specific Fitch lens for fintech-style lenders.
sub_types: Online / Digital Bank / Neobank / Fintech Bank

agency_intel: SLOOS (Q1 2026) shows credit-standard tightening in both C&I and CRE lending, with NDFI lending restrained. Useful two ways in a meeting: as evidence of sector-wide discipline when explaining underwriting posture, and as the backdrop for questions about growth running against the tightening trend — which draws scrutiny.
